message:
fcp-mesh: distinguish stale revocation pushes from invalid signatures with a dedicated MeshNodeError variant

Previously `MeshNode::handle_revocation_push` collapsed the
"timestamp outside the gossip TTL window" rejection into
`MeshNodeError::PeerSignatureInvalid { peer, message_kind:
"revocation push" }`. That is wrong in two ways:

1. The signature is valid — the push authenticated correctly against
   the peer's registered key. Emitting `PeerSignatureInvalid` sends
   operators chasing a cryptographic fault when the problem is
   actually freshness.
2. Downstream policy (e.g. "revoke peer after N invalid signatures")
   would conflate replay attempts with real signature-forgery
   attempts, skewing abuse counters.

Fix: introduce a separate
`MeshNodeError::StaleGossipMessage { peer, message_kind }` variant
and route the freshness-window rejection through it, keeping
`PeerSignatureInvalid` reserved for actual signature failures.

Changes in crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:

* New error variant:
  `StaleGossipMessage { peer: String, message_kind: &'static str }`
  with Display `"stale {message_kind} from {peer}"`. Uses the same
  `&'static str` tag convention as the other gossip variants so
  downstream log-format code (and tests) can match on
  `message_kind == "revocation push"` identically across variants.
* `handle_revocation_push`: replace the
  `Err(MeshNodeError::PeerSignatureInvalid { peer, message_kind })`
  return on `now_secs.saturating_sub(push.timestamp) >
  self.gossip.summary_ttl_secs()` with
  `Err(MeshNodeError::StaleGossipMessage { peer, message_kind })`.
* New test `handle_revocation_push_rejects_stale_message`:
  - Registers peer-1's verifying key on the node.
  - Builds a `RevocationPushMessage` with timestamp 100, signs it
    with the registered key, attaches a `NodeSignature`.
  - Calls `node.handle_revocation_push(push,
    100 + GossipConfig::default().summary_ttl_secs + 1)` — i.e. a
    now_secs that is one second past the TTL horizon relative to
    the message's timestamp.
  - Asserts the returned error matches
    `MeshNodeError::StaleGossipMessage { message_kind: "revocation
    push", .. }`. Anything else — including the previous
    `PeerSignatureInvalid` classification — fails the test.

This is strictly a classification refactor: behavior at the wire
layer (stale pushes are dropped, fresh pushes go through) is
unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
